gauge.name: Kishwaukee River near Perryville

impacts.statement: Beltline Road and Kishwaukee Road threatened south of Rockford. A runway and taxiway may be inundated at Chicago Rockford International Airport.
Industrial structures threatened along Beltline Road south of Rockford.
Commercial structures threatened along IL-251 (11th Street) near Rockford and New Milford.
Blackhawk Road threatened south of Rockford.
Widespread inundation of the floodplain reaches numerous structures along Will James Road, Schell Drive, Milton Road, Vesta Drive, Ralph Road, and Rydberg Road near Rockford and New Milford.
IL-251 (11th Street) threatened near Rockford and New Milford.
Rydberg Road threatened near Rockford and New Milford.
Residential structures threatened along Rydberg Road near Rockford and New Milford.

Residential structures threatened along Will James Road near Rockford and New Milford. Structures threatened at Rockford Christian Camp. Water nears a runway at Chicago Rockford International Airport.
Residences threatened along Schell Drive near Rockford and New Milford. Falcon Road threatened at Chicago Rockford International Airport.
Mulford Road threatened south of Rockford.
Campsites threatened at Blackhawk Valley Campground south of Rockford.
Will James Road threatened near Rockford and New Milford.
Low-lying sections of Atwood Park inundated south of Rockford.
Old Perryville Road threatened south of Rockford. Widespread inundation of agricultural land and forest land near Chicago Rockford International Airport.
Roadways threatened at Blackhawk Valley Campground south of Rockford.
Water overtops low-lying banks in Blackhawk Springs Forest Preserve and in south Rockford near Chicago Rockford International Airport. Trails threatened at Blackhawk Springs Forest Preserve.
Flooding begins of low lying agricultural lands near the river.

impactsLowWaters.impact: Approximate stage of the 1-in-10 annual chance flow (7-day average).

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past and future precipitation.  The  stages/flows that will occur may be different if future rainfall is different than forecast.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
